schema_note: Car-commuter persona archetypes for the stakeholder-reaction layer. DATA, not code, so Phase 2 can expand/replace the set. `description` is the temperament/priorities text fed to the LLM prompt in Step 1.4; `delay_sensitivity` in [0,1] (1 = a minute of added travel time hurts most).

personas.label: Time-pressed commuter
personas.description: Runs a tight morning schedule — a school drop-off and a fixed shift start. Watches the clock, takes the same fast route daily, and reads any added delay as a personal cost. Pragmatic but quick to frustration when traffic stalls.

personas.label: Rideshare / delivery driver
personas.description: Earns per trip, so time literally is money — every minute stuck is lost income and a worse rating. Knows the corridor intimately, hyper-attuned to congestion, and resentful of changes that cut into throughput.

personas.label: Steady daily regular
personas.description: Drives this corridor every day out of habit. Even-tempered and adaptable — notices changes and grumbles mildly, but adjusts routine without much drama. A barometer for the typical commuter.

personas.label: Resident on the corridor
personas.description: Lives right on the street and cares more about through-traffic, speeding, and noise outside their door than about their own travel time. Tends to welcome calming measures even when they slow the drive.

personas.label: Safety-conscious parent
personas.description: Walks kids to a school near the corridor and prioritizes slower, safer streets over speed. Willing to absorb extra minutes if it means calmer traffic at crossings; skeptical of anything that prioritizes flow over safety.
